title: UX audit and remediation proposal for CryptoSavingsTracker iOS
executive_summary: Run a wave-based UX audit and remediation program for the current iOS app, using Xcode MCP review when it can produce deterministic workspace diagnostics and preview/runtime evidence, and falling back to repo-native CLI, simulator, screenshot, and UI-test workflows when it cannot. The work closes all P0 and P1 UX issues in the dated in-scope screen snapshot, reuses the repository's existing visual-system, navigation, CloudKit, and family-sharing release gates as the only acceptance surface, and leaves behind stronger guardrails without introducing a parallel design system or trust-model rewrite.

repo_baseline.closure_boundary_rule: Definition of done is measured against the active iOS screen snapshot captured on 2026-04-04 plus any regressions introduced while the remediation is in flight.

problem.statement: The app already has strong UX governance artifacts, but they are unevenly applied across a large active SwiftUI surface. The result is a mixed baseline: token and navigation policies exist, runtime accessibility tests exist, screenshot capture scripts exist, and trust-sensitive CloudKit and family-sharing runbooks exist, yet active screens still show raw visual literals, silent logging paths, placeholder states, truncation risks, and incomplete recovery UX.
user_request_alignment: Audit current UX issues using Xcode MCP review where available.
Use an equivalent deterministic fallback when Xcode MCP is unavailable or insufficient for a target flow.
Do not stop at inventory creation; ship code-level fixes for discovered issues.

goals: Produce a complete, reviewable inventory of current iOS UX issues for the dated in-scope screen snapshot.
Fix every P0 and P1 UX issue discovered within scope before closeout.
Reuse existing repository contracts and release gates instead of creating a parallel standards track.
Prioritize trust-critical states first: loading, empty, stale, error, recovery, destructive confirmation, and dirty-dismiss behavior.
Leave stronger automated and human review guardrails behind so the same classes of issues are less likely to regress.
non_goals: No net-new product features or information architecture changes unless required to resolve a confirmed P0 or P1 UX defect.
No repo-wide architecture rewrite of already-shipped state, error, or freshness abstractions.
No CloudKit persistence-model redesign or family-sharing semantic change under the guise of UX cleanup.
No full visual rebrand or typography reset.
No Android remediation in this proposal.

inline_in_this_program: Adopt already-shipped error and degraded-state primitives where a touched screen currently omits user-facing loading, error, stale, or retry UI.
Remove silent print-only recovery paths from touched P0 and P1 screens.
Standardize screen-level recovery affordances in the audited views without changing service contracts globally.
deferred_to_related_proposal: Any repo-wide introduction or redefinition of ServiceResult, ViewState, ErrorAwareViewModel, AsyncContentView, FreshnessIndicatorView, or error translation architecture.
Any service-layer contract migration that changes semantics outside the touched screens.
related_proposal_reconciliation.deconfliction_rule: This program may consume existing abstractions and file targeted adoption-gap fixes, but it does not become the umbrella rewrite for error architecture.

inline_in_this_program: Treat Dynamic Type, touch-target sizing, VoiceOver labels, and large-text financial readability as release criteria for every P0 and P1 remediation.
Remove confirmed truncation or layout failures from the audited iOS flows in scope.
Extend runtime accessibility evidence for the touched iOS flows.
deferred_to_related_proposal: Cross-platform accessibility architecture or a broader accessibility layout-mode rollout beyond the touched iOS screens.
related_proposal_reconciliation.deconfliction_rule: This program closes confirmed iOS accessibility defects in scope, while the broader hardening proposal remains the place for cross-platform expansion and architectural generalization.

inline_in_this_program: Fix discoverability and CTA hierarchy defects inside the existing planning flow if they are causing a confirmed UX issue on a touched screen.
Improve copy, affordance clarity, and recovery messaging within the current navigation structure.
deferred_to_related_proposal: Any top-level navigation restructuring, new tabs, or primary-route changes for planning.
Any platform-wide information architecture change whose main purpose is feature discoverability rather than remediation of a current screen defect.
related_proposal_reconciliation.deconfliction_rule: This program improves the current planning flow as shipped; it does not absorb the broader navigation/IA redesign proposal.

alternatives_rejected.name: full_audit_then_fixes_later
alternatives_rejected.reason: Findings go stale, scope drifts, and user-visible improvements arrive too late.

alternatives_rejected.name: opportunistic_cleanup_only
alternatives_rejected.reason: No credible definition of done, poor traceability, and high miss risk for trust-sensitive UX defects.

preferred_approach.rationale: Wave-based audit-and-fix best matches the repository's current maturity: strong governance artifacts exist, but adoption is uneven. The fastest safe path is to audit, remediate, and certify each wave using the release machinery already in the repo.

availability_criteria: Xcode MCP can access the active workspace or project context for the target flow.
It can surface deterministic file, preview, build, or issue-navigator evidence for the target screen or interaction.
The produced evidence is stable enough to support a close or fail decision for the finding.
fallback_criteria: Xcode MCP cannot connect to the workspace or load the target context.
Preview or runtime inspection is unavailable, inconsistent, or not representative for the target flow.
The target issue is better evidenced by existing repo-native runtime UI tests, screenshot scripts, static scans, or release artifacts.
fallback_workflow: Use repo-native static scans for known debt patterns such as raw visual literals, print-only paths, EmptyView placeholders, modal annotations, and truncation risks.
Use xcodebuild and the existing XCTest/UI-test suites for runtime validation.
Use the existing screenshot capture scripts and checked-in screenshot manifests for before/after evidence.
Use the current runbooks and release bundles as the acceptance surface for navigation, visual-system, CloudKit, and family-sharing flows.
audit_methodology.close_rule: No finding closes unless it has deterministic evidence through either Xcode MCP or the documented fallback path. Inspection method is not the source of truth; the release evidence bundle is.

in_scope_flows: Family sharing owner and invitee surfaces
supporting_surfaces: Shared visual components touched by the audited flows
ViewModels whose user-visible state presentation is incomplete or inconsistent
Navigation and modal affordances touched by the audited flows
Accessibility and screenshot evidence tied to the audited flows
out_of_scope: New top-level IA or new feature work

severity.P0: Trust loss, blocked task completion, destructive ambiguity, inaccessible critical path, or data-loss-adjacent UX.
severity.P1: Recurring confusion, missing recovery path, supported-size layout failure, or inconsistent modal/destructive behavior on important flows.
severity.P2: Polish gaps and non-critical consistency debt.

within_wave_order: P0 issues in finance-critical and trust-sensitive flows
P1 issues in release-blocking financial flows
P2 piggyback fixes only when they are in the same touched screen or component, add negligible risk, and require no extra evidence package
prioritization_model.cross_wave_p0_rule: Any newly discovered P0, even if found in a later-wave flow, is pulled into the current active remediation track immediately and must be triaged before the current wave can close.

canonical_evidence_path.principle: Do not create a parallel repo-wide audit registry or alternate definition of done. Extend the existing release bundles and runbooks that already govern promotion.

visual_system_flows.usage: Planning, dashboard, settings, and other visual/state-heavy flow slices close against the existing visual-system bundle pattern.

navigation_flows.usage: Modal, dirty-dismiss, and route-policy fixes close against the existing navigation governance package and screenshot evidence.

cloudkit_and_family_sharing_flows.usage: CloudKit settings surfaces and family-sharing screens close only when the current trust runbooks and release evidence remain green.

canonical_evidence_path.dated_finding_log_rule: If a per-wave finding ledger is needed for execution discipline, it must live inside the relevant existing release bundle for that wave, not as a new top-level tracking system.

bounded_cross_cutting_program.objective: Fix adoption gaps against already-shipped shared primitives before repeating the same local repair across many screens.
allowed_targets: Adoption of existing ServiceResult, ViewState, ErrorAwareViewModel, AsyncContentView, and FreshnessIndicatorView where a touched screen already needs them
Removal of raw visual literals from touched active views in favor of the current token contract
Standardization of existing modal and toolbar behavior against NAV-MOD rules
Completion of state coverage in the current visual-state matrix for touched reusable components
Reinforcement of the current motion contract for touched finance-critical flows
explicit_exclusions: No repo-wide rewrite of all view models or services
No new parallel error-state abstraction set
No replacement of existing freshness semantics
No speculative component redesign without a confirmed adoption gap
delivery_artifacts: Adoption matrix per wave showing already compliant surfaces, extend-here surfaces, and local-fix-only surfaces
Component state matrix updates using the existing visual-state-matrix contract
Motion-language compliance notes recorded in the relevant wave evidence bundle

implementation_invariants: CloudKit remains the sole authoritative persistence truth for the cutover-protected runtime. No UX remediation changes that contract.
Invitee behavior remains read-only. This proposal does not introduce owner/invitee capability drift.
Family-sharing freshness, publish, refresh, and namespace semantics remain unchanged unless a separate design review explicitly approves a semantic change.
App-level route ownership stays with the existing coordinator and the current NavigationStack policy. No local bypasses are introduced.
All modal and dialog call sites continue to follow MOD-01 through MOD-05 and retain machine-checkable NAV-MOD annotations where applicable.
Existing rollout thresholds, telemetry contracts, and rollback expectations in the visual-system, navigation, CloudKit, and family-sharing runbooks remain authoritative.

visual_direction: Follow the existing visual token contract and calm finance-first surface policy.
Remove raw ad hoc colors in touched active views.
Do not rely on color alone for status or risk communication.
motion_and_feedback: Reuse the motion contract already defined in the visual-system documentation.
Audit screen transitions, press feedback, loading-to-recovery transitions, and reduced-motion behavior for touched flows.
Do not introduce custom animation language that conflicts with platform-native finance-critical flows.
accessibility: Dynamic Type, large-text readability, touch-target sizing, and VoiceOver semantics are release criteria for every P0 and P1 fix.
Critical amounts, labels, and CTAs must not depend on forced single-line truncation.
human_review: Each wave includes a heuristic walkthrough by design or UX on happy path, degraded path, and accessibility-sized path.
Every P0 and P1 fix requires UX review approval recorded in the wave closeout evidence.

scope: Run initial static scans and evidence collection
Map each in-scope screen to the governing release bundle and runbook
Record default decisions and wave sequencing
deliverables: Initial burndown metrics for confirmed debt signals
Adoption matrix seed for shared primitives
wave_plan.assumptions: One iOS engineer with QA automation and design review support

wave_plan.why_first: These are the repo's strongest existing governed flows, with the most mature screenshot and visual-system evidence already in place.
evidence_path: navigation artifacts for MOD-02 and dirty-dismiss where planning/settings touch modal flows
Existing visual-system bundle pattern for touched goal surfaces
Goal dashboard release gate where dashboard-specific evidence applies
Targeted screenshot and runtime accessibility evidence

scope: Verify all P0 and P1 findings are closed

hard_closure_targets.target: 100% of P0 issues in the dated scope snapshot are fixed before closeout
100% of P1 issues in the dated scope snapshot are fixed before closeout

seed_evidence_kpis.rule: No raw visual literals remain in active iOS views within the dated scope snapshot.
No print-only user-facing recovery paths remain in active iOS view files within the dated scope snapshot.
No EmptyView placeholder remains where a user-facing state should be represented in the dated scope snapshot.

seed_evidence_kpis.target: 0 occurrences on P0/P1 financial amounts, critical labels, and primary CTAs
seed_evidence_kpis.rule: Large-text validation must show no clipped or force-truncated critical financial content on remediated screens.

seed_evidence_kpis.baseline: auditCurrentScreen() returns an empty issue list stub
seed_evidence_kpis.target: stub replaced with real signal or explicitly removed from the acceptance path by the end of Wave 1

wave_acceptance_requirements: 100% before/after evidence for changed P0/P1 financial and trust-sensitive screens.
Runtime accessibility assertions and relevant UI tests stay green for the wave.
Existing visual-system UX metrics remain at or above their documented thresholds where those metrics already apply.
Family-sharing and CloudKit thresholds remain within the current runbook guardrails for Wave 4.

default_decisions.p2_scope: Default to shipping only P0 and P1 findings. P2 items are fixed inline only if they are low-risk piggybacks in a touched screen or component; otherwise they move to the residual backlog.
default_decisions.artifact_location: Do not introduce a new top-level artifact home. Repository truth remains inside the existing docs/release and docs/testing evidence packages already used by the repo's release gates.
default_decisions.branching_strategy: Default to one branch or review track per wave, with a separate pull-forward track only for newly discovered cross-wave P0 issues.
default_decisions.evidence_requirement: Before/after screenshots and runtime accessibility evidence are mandatory for changed P0/P1 financial and trust-sensitive screens. Supporting non-critical screens may close with targeted test evidence plus representative captures when layout or state changed materially.
default_decisions.override_rule: All defaults may be overridden at Phase 0 kickoff only if new repo evidence materially changes scope, cost, or gating.

rollout_and_staffing.resource_assumption: One primary iOS engineer, part-time QA automation support, and design or UX review at each wave close.
rollout_and_staffing.sequencing_note: The order is intentionally wave-oriented because the existing release evidence and risk boundaries are already wave-shaped in the repository.
cross_wave_triage_protocol: If a new P0 is discovered in a later-wave flow, pull it forward immediately into the current active track.
If a later-wave P1 blocks the current wave's user journey or evidence, escalate it into the current wave.
Otherwise keep later-wave work in its assigned wave to preserve closure discipline.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: The phrase 'fix all UX issues' becomes unbounded scope.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Bound closure to the dated 2026-04-04 scope snapshot and require full closure only for P0 and P1 findings.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Cross-cutting cleanup turns into a broad architecture rewrite.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Restrict cross-cutting work to adoption gaps against existing abstractions and document explicit exclusions.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: CloudKit or family-sharing trust behavior regresses under a generic UX pass.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Treat the existing CloudKit and family-sharing release gates as non-negotiable and isolate those flows into their own late wave.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Audit evidence goes stale while implementation proceeds.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Audit and remediate in the same wave and refresh the relevant evidence bundle before promotion.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Human-centered clarity issues are missed by code-heavy analysis.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Require heuristic walkthroughs and explicit UX review approval in every wave.

open_questions.question: Should post-ship telemetry be extended beyond the current release-gate metrics once the P0/P1 remediation pass is complete?
open_questions.default_answer: Do not block this remediation on net-new analytics. Reuse the metrics already present in visual-system and family-sharing governance first, then assess additional instrumentation after closeout.

reviewer_feedback_resolution.resolution: Resolved by replacing the parallel artifact model with explicit reuse of the existing visual-system, navigation, CloudKit, and family-sharing evidence packages as the only acceptance surface.
Resolved by rewriting the cross-cutting phase into a bounded adoption-gap program against existing primitives and explicitly excluding repo-wide rewrites.
Resolved by adding non-negotiable implementation invariants for CloudKit truth, invitee capability boundaries, family-sharing freshness semantics, and coordinator or modal-policy ownership.
Resolved by replacing directional success language with measurable KPI baselines and targets grounded in the confirmed seed evidence.
Resolved by providing default answers for P2 scope, artifact location, branching strategy, and evidence requirements, all marked as overridable only at Phase 0 kickoff.
Resolved by adding an explicit reconciliation section for the overlapping error-handling, accessibility, and planning-IA proposals.
Resolved by splitting onboarding and family sharing into separate late waves.
Resolved by adding T-shirt effort sizing and a cross-wave P0 triage protocol.
Resolved by adding motion-language compliance and component state matrix updates to the bounded cross-cutting program.
Resolved by adding a heuristic review step and UX approval requirement to every wave.

recommendation: Approve the revised proposal. It preserves the user's request for Xcode MCP-led UX review when practical, defines a deterministic fallback path when it is not, binds execution to the repository's existing release gates, and narrows 'fix all UX problems' into a credible, measurable, trust-safe remediation program.
